title: Practitioner's Playbook - AI Contract Review Workflow
subject: Do this today: The 30-minute AI contract review setup that saves 10 hours/week
preheader: Step-by-step instructions you can implement before lunch
bodyHtml:
LegalTech AI Hub

The Practitioner’s Playbook

Tactical guides you can use this week

Build an AI-Assisted Contract Review Workflow in 30 Minutes

TIME: 30 MIN · DIFFICULTY: BEGINNER · TOOLS: FREE

Most lawyers know AI can help with contract review. Almost nobody has a repeatable workflow for it. Today, you’re going to build one. By the end of this email, you’ll have a step-by-step process you can use every time a contract hits your desk.

What you’ll need:

• Access to any AI assistant (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini — any works)
• Our free Clause Checker (optional but recommended)
• A contract you’re currently reviewing (use a real one)

Step 1: Create Your Standard Prompt Template (10 minutes)

The biggest time-waster in AI-assisted review is writing new prompts from scratch every time. Create a template you reuse. Here’s a proven one:

You are reviewing a [CONTRACT TYPE] between [PARTY A] and [PARTY B].

Analyze the following contract for:
1. Unusual or non-standard clauses
2. Missing standard protections for [MY CLIENT’S ROLE]
3. Ambiguous language that could be interpreted against my client
4. Termination provisions and their practical implications
5. Indemnification scope and any carve-outs
6. Liability caps and whether they’re reasonable for this deal size

For each issue found, provide:
- The specific clause number and text
- Why it’s a concern
- Suggested alternative language

Contract text follows:
[PASTE CONTRACT]

Save this as a text snippet (TextExpander, Alfred, or even a sticky note). You’ll use it on every contract.

Step 2: Run the First Pass (5 minutes)

Paste your contract into the AI tool using the template above. Read the output. Do not trust it yet. This first pass is for issue identification, not issue resolution. You’re using AI as a spotter, not a decision-maker.

Mark each flagged issue as: Valid, False positive, or Needs deeper review.

Step 3: Clause-Level Deep Dive (10 minutes)

For every clause marked “Valid” or “Needs deeper review,” run it through the Clause Checker individually. The standalone tool often catches nuances that a full-contract review misses because it’s purpose-built for clause analysis.

Step 4: Comparison Check (5 minutes)

If you have a previous version or a standard template, use the Doc Diff tool to see exactly what changed. Focus your human attention on the deltas, not the boilerplate.

Step 5: Human Review and Sign-Off (your judgment)

Now do your normal review, but with a head start. You’ve already got a list of flagged issues, clause-level analysis, and a change comparison. Your job is to apply legal judgment to the issues AI identified. This is the part AI can’t do.

THE RULE

AI does the first pass. You do the final pass. Never reverse this. The lawyer is always the last set of eyes, never the first.

That’s it. 30 minutes to set up, and you’ll use this workflow on every contract going forward. Lawyers who’ve adopted this tell us they’re saving 8-12 hours per week on contract review. Try it this week and reply to tell me how it went.

You’re receiving this because you subscribed to LegalTech AI Hub updates.
